title: Auto-Channel Creation & Task Linking
description:
The killer feature: automatically create chat channels linked to epics/tasks and cross-post status events.

## What to implement

### Auto-channel creation in `packages/moe-daemon/src/state/StateManager.ts`:

Hook into existing methods:
- `createEpic()` → auto-create channel with name `epic-{epicId}`, type `'epic'`, linkedEntityId = epicId
- `createTask()` → auto-create channel with name `task-{taskId}`, type `'task'`, linkedEntityId = taskId
- Only when `ProjectSettings.chatAutoCreateChannels` is true
- Channel name should use a human-readable slug from the epic/task title (e.g., "epic-auth-system" not just "epic-abc123")

### System messages for task lifecycle events:

Post system messages (sender: "system") to the task's linked channel when:
- Task status changes → "Task moved to WORKING by worker-abc"
- Plan submitted → "Implementation plan submitted (N steps)"
- Plan approved → "Plan approved — ready for work"
- Plan rejected → "Plan rejected: {reason}"
- Step completed → "Step N completed: {step description}"
- QA approved → "QA approved — task complete"
- QA rejected → "QA rejected: {reason}"
- Worker blocked → post to BOTH task channel AND "general" channel: "worker-abc blocked on task-{id}: {reason}"

### Files to modify:
- `packages/moe-daemon/src/state/StateManager.ts` — Hook auto-channel creation into createEpic/createTask, add system message posting to status change handlers
- `packages/moe-daemon/src/tools/reportBlocked.ts` — Cross-post to task channel + general
- `packages/moe-daemon/src/tools/submitPlan.ts` — Post system message to task channel
- `packages/moe-daemon/src/tools/completeStep.ts` — Post system message to task channel
- `packages/moe-daemon/src/tools/completeTask.ts` — Post system message to task channel
- `packages/moe-daemon/src/tools/qaApprove.ts` — Post system message to task channel
- `packages/moe-daemon/src/tools/qaReject.ts` — Post system message to task channel
definitionOfDone: Epic creation auto-creates linked chat channel
Task creation auto-creates linked chat channel
Channel names use human-readable slugs from epic/task titles
Task status changes post system messages to task channel
Worker blocked cross-posts to task channel AND general channel

implementationPlan.description: Add a helper function slugify(title: string): string in packages/moe-daemon/src/util/mentionRouter.ts (or a new util/slugify.ts) that converts a title to a URL-safe channel name slug. Logic: lowercase, replace spaces/special chars with hyphens, truncate to 40 chars, remove trailing hyphens. Example: 'Auth System Redesign' -> 'auth-system-redesign'. Error handling: return fallback slug from the entity ID if title is empty.
implementationPlan.note: Created slugify utility: lowercase, remove special chars, spaces to hyphens, collapse/trim hyphens, truncate to 40 chars, fallback to entity ID if title empty.

implementationPlan.description: Hook auto-channel creation into StateManager.createEpic(). After the epic is created and written to disk, check if project.settings.chatAutoCreateChannels is true (default true via nullish coalescing). If so, call this.createChannel({ name: 'epic-' + slugify(epic.title), type: 'epic', linkedEntityId: epic.id }). Wrap in try/catch so channel creation failure doesn't block epic creation — log warning on error handling failure and continue.
implementationPlan.note: Hooked auto-channel creation into createEpic. Respects chatAutoCreateChannels setting (defaults true via ??). Wrapped in try/catch so channel failure doesn't block epic creation.

implementationPlan.description: Hook auto-channel creation into StateManager.createTask(). Same pattern as epic: after task is written, check chatAutoCreateChannels setting, then call this.createChannel({ name: 'task-' + slugify(task.title), type: 'task', linkedEntityId: task.id }). Same error handling: wrap in try/catch, log warning on failure.
implementationPlan.note: Hooked auto-channel creation into createTask. Same pattern as epic.

implementationPlan.description: Add a private helper method postSystemMessage(channelOrEntityId: string, content: string): Promise<void> to StateManager. This finds the channel linked to the given entityId (epic/task ID) by scanning channels Map for matching linkedEntityId, then calls sendMessage({ channel: foundChannelId, sender: 'system', content }). If no linked channel found, silently skip (error handling: no-throw). This centralizes all system message posting.
implementationPlan.note: Added postSystemMessage(entityId, content) — finds linked channel by entityId and posts system message. Also added postToGeneral(content) for cross-posting blocked events. Both methods never throw.

implementationPlan.description: Add system message posting to StateManager.updateTask() for status changes. After the task is updated and event emitted, if the task status changed, call postSystemMessage(task.id, 'Task moved to {newStatus} by {assignedWorkerId || "unknown"}'). This covers all status transitions in one place. Error handling: wrap in try/catch, never let system message failure block the actual task update.
implementationPlan.note: Added system message posting in updateTask for status changes. Fires after emit, never blocks the update. Uses .catch(() => {}) for fire-and-forget.

implementationPlan.description: Add system message posting to existing MCP tool handlers. In submitPlan.ts: after successful plan submission, call state.postSystemMessage(taskId, 'Implementation plan submitted ({N} steps)'). In completeStep.ts: call state.postSystemMessage(taskId, 'Step {N} completed: {description}'). In completeTask.ts: call state.postSystemMessage(taskId, 'Task submitted for review'). In qaApprove.ts: call state.postSystemMessage(taskId, 'QA approved — task complete'). In qaReject.ts: call state.postSystemMessage(taskId, 'QA rejected: {reason}'). All calls wrapped in try/catch for error handling — tool should succeed even if system message fails.
implementationPlan.note: Added system message posting to all 5 tool handlers. All wrapped in try/catch to never block the tool operation.

implementationPlan.description: Add cross-posting for blocked workers in reportBlocked.ts. After updating worker status, call state.postSystemMessage(taskId, 'worker-{id} blocked: {reason}') for the task channel. Also find the 'general' channel (by type or name) and post the same message there. Error handling: wrap both calls in try/catch independently so failure of one doesn't block the other.
implementationPlan.note: Added cross-posting for blocked workers: posts to both task channel (via postSystemMessage) and general channel (via postToGeneral). Independent try/catch for each.

implementationPlan.description: Test: Run 'cd packages/moe-daemon && npm run build' to verify compilation. Manually test by creating an epic via MCP and verifying a linked channel appears in .moe/channels/. Create a task, verify its channel. Submit a plan, verify system message appears in .moe/messages/{task-channel}.jsonl.
implementationPlan.note: Fixed event emitter test to expect 3 events (TASK_CREATED + CHANNEL_CREATED + TASK_UPDATED) instead of 2, due to auto-channel creation on createTask. All 293 tests pass.
